title: gh-119118: Fix performance regression in tokenize module
author: lysnikolaou
body:
- Cache line object to avoid creating a Unicode object for all of the tokens in the same line.
- Speed up byte offset to column offset conversion by using the smallest buffer possible to measure the difference.


Where: [X.Y] is the branch name, e.g. [3.6].

GH-NNNN refers to the PR number from `main`.

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body: Another idea: we do have the token already as unicode (`str`) so if the line has not changed we can keep adding the size of the token itself to our state and take into account the number of whitespace characters between the tokens (but this is always ASCII so we can basically just calculate as the diff of two pointers).

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body: I'm discussing another fix with @lysnikolaou offline

comments.author: pablogsal
comments.body: The docs failure seems unrelated. @hugovk do we know what may be happening here?

comments.author: lysnikolaou
comments.body:
The latest results are:

```
cpython on  performance-tokenize [$] via C v15.0.0-clang via 🐍 v3.11.3 
❯ python tmp/t.py
cpython darwin 3.11.3 (main, May  8 2023, 13:16:43) [Clang 14.0.3 (clang-1403.0.22.14.1)]
Time taken: 0.5428769588470459

cpython on  performance-tokenize [$] via C v15.0.0-clang via 🐍 v3.11.3 
❯ ./python.exe tmp/t.py
cpython darwin 3.14.0a0 (heads/performance-tokenize-dirty:ab3437096a7, May 28 2024, 19:26:19) [Clang 15.0.0 (clang-1500.3.9.4)]
Time taken: 0.4570140838623047
```

The test failures appear to be unrelated. We can probably merge this.

comments.author: hugovk
comments.body:
> The docs failure seems unrelated. @hugovk do we know what may be happening here?

Yep, need to merge in latest `main`, the new option has been added there (re: https://github.com/python/cpython/pull/119221).
